title: Conspiracy theories — Trudeau/Castro, Bill Gates, post-Covid taste buds
html:
Conspiracy theories — Trudeau/Castro, Bill Gates, post-Covid taste buds

Source: 2025-02-15 Apple Note. A four-line scratch of bar-talk-grade conspiracies Yahir was kicking around in late winter Grants Pass. Pure genre — meant for himself, never an argument.

TL;DR

  • "Justin Trudeau is Fidel Castro['s] son" — recycles the long-running internet rumor; Yahir parks it without comment.
  • "Bill Gates extended and exaggerated severity of Covid to elongate and not pay his divorce" — the only line with a motive attached, links the pandemic era to the Gates divorce timeline.
  • "Chicken bakes ain't the same" — likely a Costco-product complaint, sitting weirdly next to global-figure conspiracies.
  • "Since Covid everyone's taste buds have been permabanned" — actually a real post-viral anosmia/parosmia phenomenon, dressed as a meme.
  • The note is shorter than its title — a single late-night impulse to capture before forgetting.

Key quote

"Since Covid everyone's taste buds have been permabanner."

Pattern observed

This is the shitpost variant of the note as letter to self genre — same medium, same one-shot capture instinct, but the target audience is "future Yahir who wants a laugh," not "future Yahir who needs reminding." Sits alongside 2024 09 12 never forget do whatever's seriousness as the other end of the Apple Notes register.

Open questions

  • Was this prompted by a specific conversation or news cycle (mid-Feb 2025 = Trudeau resignation noise)?
  • The chicken-bake line — Costco specifically? The post-Covid taste-bud theory may secretly be the real complaint.

Related

  • bill gates
  • covid pandemic era
  • — the serious counter-genre
  • grants pass or
